ambiguities.description: What entities, relationships, and boundaries define this system?
How do users and processes interact with the system over time?
What rules, constraints, and compliance requirements apply?
What external systems, integrations, and deployment constraints exist?
What defines MVP, done, and out-of-scope for this construction?
Source systems and their data formats, APIs, and access patterns
Schema and contracts (explicit schema, schema-on-read, evolution policy)
Validation rules and data quality requirements
Freshness/SLA requirements for different data products
Monitoring and alerting for pipeline health

questions.question: What does success look like for this system?
Who are the primary users and what is the organization size?
What is the timeline and what constraints (budget, compliance, team) apply?
What are the source systems and their data formats?
What schema and evolution policy is required?
What data quality and validation rules apply?
What freshness/SLA is required for each data product?
What are the retention and privacy requirements?
How should backfills and reprocessing be handled?

assumptions.assumption: Source systems are available and documented
Standard monitoring and alerting are sufficient for MVP

suggested_closures.decision: Pipeline construction will separate ingestion, validation, transformation, and serving into distinct stages.
suggested_closures.rationale: Separation enables independent scaling, retry, and evolution of each stage.

seed_tasks.title: Define system scope and success criteria
seed_tasks.transformation: Document what the system must do, for whom, and what success looks like.
seed_tasks.evidence_requirement: A written scope document exists and has been reviewed.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: A third party can understand the scope without asking clarifying questions.

seed_tasks.title: Identify constraints and non-goals
seed_tasks.transformation: Explicitly list budget, timeline, compliance, and out-of-scope items.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: No hidden constraints remain that would invalidate the construction plan.

seed_tasks.title: Inventory source systems and data contracts
seed_tasks.transformation: List all sources, their formats, schemas, access methods, and owners.
seed_tasks.evidence_requirement: Source inventory with data contracts exists.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every source has a documented schema and access method.

seed_tasks.title: Define ingestion mode and schema policy
seed_tasks.transformation: Choose batch vs streaming and schema evolution policy.
seed_tasks.evidence_requirement: Ingestion and schema policy is documented.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Policy covers schema changes without breaking downstream consumers.

seed_tasks.title: Define data quality and validation rules
seed_tasks.transformation: Document validation rules, quality thresholds, and failure handling.
seed_tasks.evidence_requirement: Quality rules and thresholds are documented.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every data product has defined quality criteria.

residuals.description: System scope and success criteria are not yet defined by the principal.
Constraints, compliance requirements, and non-goals have not been documented.
Source systems and data contracts are not defined.
Schema and evolution policy is undefined.
